summary:
For this high-risk scenario involving tooth extractions and pre-existing heart conditions, amoxicillin serves optimally because it provides necessary bacteriostatic protection against potential infections like those causing bacterial endocarditis, fitting well within standard medical guidelines despite interactions with current therapies. \boxed{Amoxicillin}

The summary concisely captures why amoxicillin was selected based primarily on its efficacy profile relative to preventing infection post-extraction alongside considerations about drug-drug interaction risks present from ongoing treatments. It maintains focus strictly upon clinically pertinent aspects addressing both therapeutic necessity and practical constraints posed by concomitant therapy regimens.
